OK lads, this is a big thicc fatboy.   Could call it a bent billiard, could call it a bullmoose, could call it a ridiculous author, call it what you will.   It is a chunky monkey.  Handmades were produced in the 1947 to 1955 range.    This has the clover on the top.    The stem is in great shape, just a bit of wear on the button, but otherwise gorgeous.   The stummel is nearly flawless, just a beauty.   Ok, thats the good.   Here is the less good.    When I bought this years ago, there were a couple of fissures that were not disclosed.    I noticed them when I got it but I did not make a big deal out of it.    They have not propagated in the time I have smoked this.    There is one that you can probably see in the photos around the four oclock position.   There is another very small one around the 11 oclock position but you probably cannot see them from the photos.      I am just letting you know that they are there and there is some risk that they could propagate, but I have not seen it since I have owned it.    You have been warned, but I dont think its a big issue on this pipe.  There is a lot of mass with those incredibly thick walls to soak up heat.   The  other thing you will notice is that the ball has been cut off.    Does not really impact the smoke for me, but does impact collectability.    Pricing this is a bit of a moving target.  It is old, it has beautiful grain, including birdseye on the foot.  But it does have a few flaws.   Price drop to

OK lads, this is a big thicc fatboy.   Could call it a bent billiard, could call it a bullmoose, could call it a ridiculous author, call it what you will.   It is a chunky monkey.  Handmades were produced in the 1947 to 1955 range.    This has the clover on the top.    The stem is in great shape, just a bit of wear on the button, but otherwise gorgeous.   The stummel is nearly flawless, just a beauty.   Ok, thats the good.   Here is the less good.    When I bought this years ago, there were a couple of fissures that were not disclosed.    I noticed them when I got it but I did not make a big deal out of it.    They have not propagated in the time I have smoked this.    There is one that you can probably see in the photos around the four oclock position.   There is another very small one around the 11 oclock position but you probably cannot see them from the photos.      I am just letting you know that they are there and there is some risk that they could propagate, but I have not seen it since I have owned it.    You have been warned, but I dont think its a big issue on this pipe.  There is a lot of mass with those incredibly thick walls to soak up heat.   The  other thing you will notice is that the ball has been cut off.    Does not really impact the smoke for me, but does impact collectability.    Pricing this is a bit of a moving target.  It is old, it has beautiful grain, including birdseye on the foot.  But it does have a few flaws.   Price drop to
